title: rubus menkei subsp. muricatus
intro: Rubus menkei subsp. muricatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble or thorny shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Rubus menkei subsp. muricatus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects that may help protect against various diseases. Rubus menkei subsp. muricatus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements and topical applications.
